title: Fix exp_fast SIMD dispatch so it auto-vectorizes without target-cpu=native

comments.author: phase-4-coordinator
comments.content: Plan: Route exp_fast_batch_f64 and exp_fast_batch_f32 through pulp::WithSimd dispatch so LLVM auto-vectorizes within per-ISA compilation. Currently exp_fast bypasses pulp entirely — the batch function is a plain scalar loop called via unary_slice_op_f64. Fix: create ExpFastF64Op/ExpFastF32Op WithSimd impls, replace the direct batch calls in explog.rs with pulp-dispatched versions. Ref: GitHub issue dollspace-gay/ferray#6

comments.author: phase-4-coordinator
comments.content: Decision: Add ExpFastF64Op/ExpFastF32Op as WithSimd impls in dispatch.rs with dedicated dispatch_exp_fast_f64/f32 functions. Modify exp_fast in explog.rs to call these instead of unary_slice_op_f64/f32. The WithSimd impl just loops calling exp_fast_f64 with #[inline(always)] — pulp compiles the with_simd body for each ISA tier (SSE2, AVX2, AVX-512), so LLVM auto-vectorizes the AVX2+ versions. No explicit SIMD intrinsics needed.
